title: Generated graph artifacts use compact references
summary: Kage generated graph artifacts should avoid duplicating canonical structural data. code graph/graph.json is a compact compatibility artifact that references structural files/symbols/imports, graph/graph.json references s
body:
Kage generated graph artifacts should avoid duplicating canonical structural data. code_graph/graph.json is a compact compatibility artifact that references structural files/symbols/imports, graph/graph.json references split memory graph artifacts, and structural cache lives in packed structural/file-cache.json with legacy per-file cache migration on refresh. Verified by npm --prefix mcp test and a 20k-file refresh reducing .agent_memory from 150M to 59M.
Evidence: Implemented compact code graph and memory graph artifacts, packed structural file-cache.json, legacy migration, viewer hydration, docs, and tests.
Verified by: npm --prefix mcp test; node mcp/dist/cli.js refresh --project /private/tmp/kage-20k-bench-1778305276164 --json
